description: Advanced-level evaluation of a travel agent bot built on Kore.ai XO Platform. Eight advanced tasks covering: contextual interruption handling, entity amendment via sub-intents, Spanish language enablement, custom real-time dashboard, lost baggage digital form, PII redaction in analytics, NPS feedback survey, and channel publishing.

llm_as_user.description: Agentic evaluation mode requires an LLM runtime (e.g. Claude API) to conduct conversations dynamically per turn. This is a new dependency — not part of the original EvalAutomaton webhook-only architecture. Must be scoped and approved before this manifest can be used in production.
llm_as_user.fallback: If LLM-as-user is unavailable, evaluation degrades to state-assertion-only mode: POST/GET/PUT/DELETE calls are confirmed via API logs and state endpoints. Conversational sub-scores (confirmation ordering, amendment handling) are held pending and flagged for manual evaluator review.

on_restart_event.penalty_label: Task required a restart during evaluation run
on_restart_event.rationale: DialogGPT and LLM behaviour is outside the candidate's direct control. One retry is granted. A second restart within the same task scores the task as incomplete.

required_nodes.label: Welcome greeting message displayed to user

tasks.candidate_instructions: Configure a Welcome Chat Flow that triggers when a user starts a conversation. Display a greeting message to the user. Present the available services using a List Entity. Based on the user's selection, automatically trigger the appropriate dialog task. The menu must cover: booking a flight, fetching a booking, modifying a booking, and FAQs.

sub_scores.label: Greeting message is displayed to the user
List Entity correctly triggers the appropriate dialog task on selection

interruption_config.resume_phrase: Resuming your previous task: Book a Flight.

scenarios.candidate_hint: Configure a global interruption handler that detects a weather intent. On detection, call a mock weather API and respond with the weather result. After responding, prompt the user to resume the booking task.

scenarios.trigger_utterance: Can I book a trip for someone else?
scenarios.candidate_hint: Configure a global interruption handler that detects the FAQ intent 'booking for someone else'. Respond with the relevant FAQ answer. After responding, prompt the user to resume the booking task.

tasks.candidate_instructions: Enable global interruption handling across all dialog tasks. When the user asks 'What’s the weather in Delhi?' mid-booking, the bot must call a mock weather API and respond with the weather result. When the user asks 'Can I book a trip for someone else?' mid-booking, the bot must respond with the relevant FAQ answer. After handling either interruption, the bot must say: “Resuming your previous task: Book a Flight.”

sub_scores.label: Global interruption rule is enabled in the bot configuration
Bot handles weather query mid-booking and calls the mock weather API
Bot says the required resume phrase after the weather interruption
Bot handles FAQ query mid-booking with a correct answer
Bot says the required resume phrase after the FAQ interruption

required_entities.semantic_hint: contact phone number including country code
date of travel in DD-MM-YYYY format

amendment_config.amendment_utterance_template: Change my date of travel {original_value} to {amended_value}
amendment_config.detection_pattern: confirm|summary|details are|you have provided|is this correct|shall i proceed
amendment_config.note: The amendment utterance specifies both the old and new value as a sub-intent. The bot must process this directly — no re-prompting and no separate confirmation step for the amendment itself.

tasks.candidate_instructions: This task evaluates sub-intent entity amendment. After the evaluator has provided all 7 booking entities, they will say 'Change my date of travel [original date] to [new date]'. The bot must process this change immediately — no re-prompting for the date, no confirmation step for the amendment itself. Incomplete inputs must prompt for only the missing detail. Ensure the Agent Node is configured to handle direct sub-intent amendments without restarting the entity collection flow.

sub_scores.label: Bot recognises 'Change my date of travel X to Y' as a direct amendment (no re-prompt)
Bot does not add a separate confirmation step for the amendment itself
Amended travel date (not original) saved in backend state after submission

language_config.coverage_requirement: All dialogs, FAQs, and response flows must be available in Spanish with consistent tone and formatting.

tasks.candidate_instructions: Enable Spanish as a supported language. All dialogs, FAQs, and response flows must be translated into Spanish with consistent tone and formatting. Users must be able to switch between English and Spanish seamlessly during the conversation — when the user writes in Spanish the bot must respond in Spanish, and vice versa.

sub_scores.label: Spanish (es) configured as a supported language in bot settings
Bot responds in Spanish when user sends a Spanish utterance
Bot follows mid-conversation language switch without re-prompt or error

required_metrics.label: Count of incoming and outgoing messages per language (English, Spanish)
Number of dialog tasks handled, categorised by date
Number of FAQs handled, categorised by date

dashboard_config.note: Data visualisation format is not restricted to charts or tables. The dashboard must refresh in real time.

tasks.candidate_instructions: Create a dashboard widget that shows: (1) count of incoming and outgoing messages per language (English, Spanish); (2) number of dialog tasks handled categorised by date; (3) number of FAQs handled categorised by date. Data visualisation format is not restricted. The dashboard must refresh in real time.

sub_scores.label: Dashboard widget present and accessible in analytics
Widget shows incoming and outgoing message counts split by language
Widget shows dialog task count categorised by date
Widget shows FAQ count categorised by date

fields.label: Email Address
fields.tooltip: For status updates on the complaint

fields.label: Baggage Tag Number (if known)
fields.tooltip: Found on the printed baggage receipt

conditional_visibility.hide_when: user selects 'I don’t know my Baggage Tag Number'

fields.label: Description of Bag
fields.tooltip: Color, size, brand, and any identifier

api_submission.endpoint_hint: mock API — grievance / lost baggage endpoint
api_submission.on_failure: Display a failure message with retry option

post_submission.description: Connect the user to the AI Agent after form submission

tasks.candidate_instructions: Upon detecting the lost baggage intent, trigger a digital form titled 'Issue Report Form' with the 8 specified fields. Apply a conditional visibility rule: if the user indicates they do not know their Baggage Tag Number, hide that field. Implement field-level validation. After submission, POST the data to the mock API and show a success or failure confirmation message. After form completion, initiate an agent handoff. Candidates are encouraged to enhance the form beyond the reference fields — the assessment rewards meaningful form design with validation, conditional visibility, and API-driven submission.

sub_scores.label: Issue Report Form triggered when lost baggage intent is detected
All 8 required form fields present with correct types and tooltips
Baggage Tag Number field hidden when user selects 'I don’t know'
Invalid field inputs rejected with appropriate re-prompt
POST API called on submission and success/failure confirmation shown
Agent handoff initiated after form completion

redaction_rules.description: Last 3 digits visible, all preceding digits masked
Last 4 digits visible, all preceding digits masked

tasks.candidate_instructions: Redact phone numbers and credit card details in chat history and analytics. The actual values must remain visible in the active conversation — redaction applies only to stored history and analytics views. Masking format: phone number — ***123 (last 3 digits visible); credit card — ****5678 (last 4 digits visible).

sub_scores.label: PII Redaction is enabled at the bot level
Phone number masked as ***XXX (last 3 digits visible) in stored history
Credit card masked as ****XXXX (last 4 digits visible) in stored history
Active conversation shows unmasked values — redaction applies only to stored data

survey_flow.question: On a scale of 0 to 10, how likely are you to recommend WanderPal to a friend or colleague?
Please share any feedback or suggestions you may have.

survey_flow.content: Thank you! Your feedback helps us improve.

tasks.candidate_instructions: Define a feedback survey using the Net Promoter Score (NPS) model. Configure it to trigger automatically at the end of these specific dialog tasks: Booking Confirmation, Cancellation Completed, Grievance Resolution. Survey flow: (1) Bot asks 'On a scale of 0 to 10, how likely are you to recommend WanderPal to a friend or colleague?'; (2) Optional follow-up text field: 'Please share any feedback or suggestions you may have.'; (3) Bot closes with: 'Thank you! Your feedback helps us improve.' Collected feedback must appear in the Feedback Dashboard under Analytics and must be downloadable as a report.

sub_scores.label: NPS survey triggers automatically at the end of Booking Confirmation dialog
Bot asks the correct NPS scale question (0–10)
Optional feedback text field offered after scale response
Closing thank-you message matches required text exactly
Collected feedback appears in Feedback Dashboard under Analytics
Feedback report is downloadable from the dashboard

publishing_config.note: Feature behaviour must be consistent across channels — no channel-specific customisation allowed. Candidates may publish on Telegram OR via a webhook endpoint.

tasks.candidate_instructions: Publish the bot on Telegram or via a webhook. Feature behaviour must be consistent across channels — no channel-specific customisation is allowed. Ensure the bot responds correctly on the published channel with the same behaviour as the development channel.

sub_scores.label: At least one accepted channel (Telegram or webhook) is published and configured
Bot responds correctly on the published channel — no channel-specific deviations

required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: An e-ticket is a digital airline ticket stored electronically in the airline's reservation system. It replaces a traditional paper ticket and can be retrieved using your booking reference number.
alternate_questions: Can you explain what an electronic ticket is?

required_faqs.primary_question: How do I know my reservation was booked?
required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: Once your booking is confirmed, you will receive a booking reference number. You can verify your reservation at any time by providing your registered contact number to the travel agent.
alternate_questions: How will I get confirmation of my booking?
What happens after I complete my booking?

required_faqs.primary_question: How do I get my e-ticket details?
required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: You can access your e-ticket details by selecting 'Fetch Booking' and providing your registered mobile number. Your complete booking information will be displayed.
alternate_questions: Where can I find my e-ticket?
How do I access my ticket information?

required_faqs.primary_question: How do I print my ticket?
required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: To print your ticket, retrieve your booking details using your mobile number, then select the print option. You can also visit the airline's website and enter your booking reference to download and print your e-ticket.
alternate_questions: Can I get a printed copy of my ticket?
How do I get a physical copy of my boarding pass?

required_faqs.primary_question: What is the latest time I can cancel my flight?
required_faqs.ground_truth_answer: You can cancel your flight up to 24 hours before the scheduled departure time. Cancellations made within 24 hours of departure may be subject to airline cancellation fees.
alternate_questions: How late can I cancel my booking?
Is there a deadline for flight cancellation?

functional_verification.note: faq1 and faq5 selected for live verification — one informational, one policy-based, representing the spread of FAQ types.

compliance_checks.label: DialogGPT is enabled
compliance_checks.tooltip: DialogGPT must be enabled for the Agent Node to function correctly. Critical — a bot with DialogGPT disabled cannot pass the Advanced assessment.

compliance_checks.label: PII Redaction is enabled
compliance_checks.tooltip: PII Redaction must be enabled at the bot level. Advanced assessment requires candidates to demonstrate awareness of data privacy — personal data collected during conversations must be redacted in logs.

compliance_checks.label: Spanish language configured
compliance_checks.tooltip: The bot must have Spanish (es) configured as a supported language. Multi-language support is a requirement of the Advanced assessment. Non-critical — reduces score but does not automatically fail.

compliance_checks.label: At least one channel published
compliance_checks.tooltip: The bot must have at least one channel published (e.g. Web, MS Teams, WhatsApp). Channel publishing demonstrates the candidate has moved beyond development mode. Non-critical — reduces score but does not automatically fail.

scoring_config.note: Tasks sum to 75 pts. FAQ (structural 10 + functional 5) = 15 pts. Compliance = 10 pts. Total = 100 pts. Advanced tasks that are currently UNTESTABLE (INTERRUPTION, LANGUAGE, FORM, SURVEY patterns) will score via evaluator override until the agentic runtime is available.

evaluator_override_fields.supporting_evidence: string (optional — paste transcript excerpt or note manual observation)

state_seeding_config.note: Pre-test seed and post-test teardown endpoints must be configured to prevent state bleed between evaluation runs.

tooltips.text: Agent Node — uses LLM to collect entities conversationally. Supports co-referencing (multiple entities in one message) and amendment (correcting a value mid-conversation). Validation rules and booking instructions should be configured here.
Service Node — makes API calls to external systems (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). Add a bookmark explaining the purpose of each Service Node.
Entity Node — collects a specific piece of information from the user. For menu navigation, use a List Entity type.
Message Node — displays information to the user. Used for confirmations, summaries, and error messages.
Confirmation Node — asks the user to confirm a value or action before proceeding.
Prompt Node — displays captured details and asks the user to confirm before the dialog proceeds to an API call.

notes: Travel Agent Advanced assessment — v2.0. Completely redesigned from Basic — evaluates 8 advanced capabilities beyond the booking flow: global interruption handling, sub-intent entity amendment, Spanish language enablement, custom real-time dashboard, digital lost baggage form (with conditional visibility and agent handoff), PII redaction, NPS feedback survey, and channel publishing. Tasks sum to 75 pts; FAQ 15 pts; compliance 10 pts = 100 pts total. Pass threshold: 75. INTERRUPTION, LANGUAGE, FORM, and SURVEY pattern executors are currently UNTESTABLE pending agentic runtime approval — evaluator override is enabled for those tasks.
